article_items.description: The platform is building on its single format to include some new iterations that give advertisers more optionality over their appearances.

Editor’s note: This article has been updated to reflect the additional details about the CTA dynamic ads that are now being tested within the ads manager.
The new formats, while minor updates to that design, give marketers more control over how their brands appear and, for the first time, a direct response element within the ad unit itself.

One exec, who currently has advertisers participating both directly and via OpenAI partners, said there are two big wish list items they want to see: scale and the ability to optimize toward business outcomes, such...

It opens the platform to the long tail of advertisers - the mid-market brands, the performance shops and the agencies testing on behalf of clients who aren’t yet ready to commit big sums - and dramatically increases...
Bigger changes to the actual ads are on the way.
It’s also introducing a dedicated e-commerce format that can be displayed in either portrait or landscape orientation and pulls in shopping data including price and customer reviews.
The portrait version is designed to stack, potentially allowing three or four ads to sit side-by-side, opening the door to a carousel-style placement.
Google announced a new souped up suite of agentic ad tools backed by its LLM, Gemini.
With enough live data now flowing through the platform, OpenAI appears to be amassing the breadth of signal needed to act on that.

article_items.title: Announcing OpenAI-compatible API support for Amazon SageMaker AI endpoints | Amazon Web Services
article_items.description: Today, Amazon SageMaker AI introduces OpenAI-compatible API support for real-time inference endpoints. If you use the OpenAI SDK, LangChain, or Strands Agents, you can now invoke models on SageMaker AI by changing only your endpoint URL. You don’t need a custom client, a SigV4 wrapper, or code rewrites. Overview With this launch, SageMaker AI endpoints
article_items.excerpt: Today, Amazon SageMaker AI introduces OpenAI-compatible API support for real-time inference endpoints. If you use the OpenAI SDK, LangChain, or Strands Agents, you can now invoke models on SageMaker AI by changing only your endpoint URL. You don’t need a custom client, a SigV4 wrapper, or code rewrites. Overview With this launch, SageMaker AI endpoints
article_items.summary: Overview With this launch, SageMaker AI endpoints Today, Amazon SageMaker AI introduces OpenAI-compatible API support for real-time inference endpoints. The practical impact: If you use the OpenAI SDK, LangChain, or Strands Agents, you can now invoke models on SageMaker AI by changing only your endpoint URL. You don’t need a custom client, a SigV4 wrapper, or code rewrites. key_points: Overview With this launch, SageMaker AI endpoints
Today, Amazon SageMaker AI introduces OpenAI-compatible API support for real-time inference endpoints.
If you use the OpenAI SDK, LangChain, or Strands Agents, you can now invoke models on SageMaker AI by changing only your endpoint URL.
You don’t need a custom client, a SigV4 wrapper, or code rewrites.
